identity.name: dk-synology-mcp
identity.description: Comprehensive Synology NAS management through the Model Context Protocol — 87 tools across 15 service categories

purpose: MCP server providing 87 tools for managing Synology NAS: file operations, downloads, backups, Docker, photos, VMs, snapshots, scheduled tasks, users/groups, shared folders, packages, cloud sync, active backup, and system monitoring. Multi-NAS support (up to 9 units).

status.summary: All 87 tools verified against live DS923+ on DSM 7. Method name audit complete. Task handling, shared folders, and system tools fixed.

structure.src/synology_mcp/tools/: 15 tool files — filestation, sysinfo, downloadstation, cloudsync, backup, docker_tools, task_scheduler, photos, package, users_groups, shares, virtualization, snapshot, active_backup, system_tools

stdio.notes: Default transport — used by Claude Desktop and Claude Code via MCP config

interfaces.name: Synology MCP
interfaces.summary: 87 MCP tools across 15 categories: file (16), system (6), download (8), cloudsync (5), backup (5), docker (8), tasks (5), photos (4), packages (4), users (4), shares (3), VMs (5), snapshots (4), active backup (6), system tools (4)

tools.name: synology-mcp
tools.purpose: Synology NAS management via Model Context Protocol

security.auth_model: NAS credentials via environment variables, optional 2FA OTP
trust_boundaries: NAS credentials stored in .env or Claude Desktop config — never committed
Admin or delegated admin account required on each NAS
HTTPS with optional cert verification (self-signed common on home NAS)

hard_rules: Every tool accepts optional 'nas' parameter — default NAS used if omitted
